articles.title: Launch Angle, Spin Loft, and the 15 Yards You're Leaving on the Tee
articles.subtitle: The distance you’re missing isn’t hiding in your swing speed. It’s hiding in the gap between your dynamic loft and your angle of attack—and most golfers have never heard of it.
articles.excerpt: A forensic examination of blade, mallet, and zero-torque designs through the lens of oblique impact mechanics. Why face angle dominates direction, what MOI really buys you, and why the dimpled ball puts a ceiling on all of it.

articles.title: What Your Putter Actually Does and Does Not Do at Impact
articles.subtitle: A forensic look at the physics of blade, mallet, and zero-torque designs. The math does not care about marketing.
articles.excerpt: A forensic examination of blade, mallet, and zero-torque designs through the lens of oblique impact mechanics. Why face angle dominates direction, what MOI really buys you, and why the dimpled ball puts a ceiling on all of it.
